subgoals.claim: Separate and adjudicate the three inequivalent conventions permitted by the wording: repetitions allowed; each list internally distinct but possibly overlapping; and all 2n points pairwise distinct. Do not transfer a conclusion between conventions without proof.

Formalize the locked asymptotic as F_2(n) = o(n/sqrt(log n)) and verify every derived threshold against this denominator, correcting all occurrences where the scouts used log n instead of sqrt(log n).

Under each distinctness convention, certify the two-anchor bound and the fixed-radius incidence argument, including treatment of distance zero, to obtain the strongest currently justified universal planar lower bound K = Ω(n^(2/3)).

For the interpretation asking for the same numerical bound in R^3, give an all-n construction with 2n pairwise-distinct points and O(n^(2/3)) cross-distances, and separately record that this does not by itself resolve an F_3 = o(f_3) comparison.

Prove, with exact hypotheses, the required upper and lower estimates for norm values in balanced square or checkerboard lattice boxes, so that the asserted Θ(n/sqrt(log n)) benchmark is available as a proved lemma rather than an imported heuristic.

Build exact finite tests that use integer or algebraic coefficient representations, quotient by row, column, color, and Euclidean symmetries, and reject candidates using two-anchor multiplicities, centered-rank constraints, and exact Euclidean realization equations.

Establish or refute the existence of positive-definite binary quadratic forms Q_m, possibly with rational translations or congruence-restricted difference boxes, for which a balanced m by m parameter box produces o(m^2/sqrt(log m)) distinct values while retaining Θ(m^2) distinct planar points.

Establish or refute a family of conjugation-stable number fields K_d and injective coefficient boxes B_L subset K_d for which the relative norm set {z conjugate(z): z in B_L-B_L-tau} has size o(L^(2d)/sqrt(2d log L)); in a bound C_d L^(2d)/sqrt(log L), this requires C_d sqrt(d) tending to zero together with uniform onset control.

Establish or refute the existence of k-symbol n by n matrices with k = o(n/sqrt(log n)) whose doubly centered matrix has rank at most two and which satisfy all positive-definite Euclidean compatibility and point-distinctness equations.

Using the exact identity between isosceles triples and weighted incidences on perpendicular bisectors, prove a structural dichotomy for configurations with K much smaller than n: either obtain T(X,Y) = O(n^2 sqrt(log n)), or force quantitatively organized partial reflection axes stronger than the mere existence of lines containing Ω(n/K) points.

Establish or refute a multiscale radial-support theorem strong enough to imply K = Ω(n/sqrt(log n)); a sufficient but potentially overstrong target is the universal equal-distance energy bound E(X,Y) = O(n^3 sqrt(log n)).

Prove the exact monotonicity and interpolation lemma needed to convert constructions on a subsequence N_t into constructions for every sufficiently large n, explicitly quantifying the permitted growth of N_(t+1)/N_t and preserving the little-o estimate.

Treat F = o(f) as a separate subproblem: define F and f under matching distinctness conventions, prove the unconditional comparison F(2n) <= f(2n), and determine which proved upper or lower bounds on f are necessary for either asymptotic implication.

Produce a final adjudication of every locked component without changing quantifiers: record the outcome under each distinctness convention, resolve the planar pairwise-distinct question by either a certified construction or a uniform lower bound, include the R^3 outcome, and separately resolve or explicitly isolate the F = o(f) question.
